steps.title: Find the antecedent
steps.content: The blank precedes "cities" — a plural noun. The previous sentence mentions "Both Nakuru, Kenya, and Ibarra, Ecuador" — two cities. The demonstrative must be plural and match "cities".

steps.title: Apply demonstrative agreement
steps.content:
Rule: demonstrative adjectives agree in number with the noun they modify. "Cities" is plural. "Both" in the prior sentence signals two referents.
  • Choice A — these: plural demonstrative — agrees with plural "cities" and correctly points back to the two cities just named. ✓
  • Choice B — that: singular demonstrative — does not agree with plural "cities". ✗
  • Choice C — this: singular demonstrative — does not agree with plural "cities". ✗
  • Choice D — each: distributive adjective meaning "one at a time" — implies separation rather than a shared quality, and doesn't work naturally with "cities basically sit right on the equator". ✗
That confirms A is correct.
